description: Peter's conversational callbacks - investing wisdom that applies to life over time.

callbacks.context: When user doubts their path or wants to change strategy

variations: Stay the course. Panic has cost more investors than any crash.
The path is working even when you can't see it. Trust the process.
Discipline is most important when it's hardest. Stay the course.
Stay the course. We've talked about this.
Remember what I said about panic? Same applies here.
The course we set together. It's still working. Trust it.

callbacks.context: When user is impatient for results

variations: Time is your greatest ally. Compounding works in everything.
The magic of compounding. It works for money, habits, relationships.
Give it time. The numbers always win in the end.
Compounding. Remember? It's working even now.
Time is still on your side. We've discussed this.
The compounding we talked about? It hasn't stopped.

callbacks.context: When discussing costs, fees, or expensive choices

variations: Watch the costs. They compound against you.
High fees are a guaranteed drag. In investing and in life.
The biggest returns come from minimizing friction.
Costs. We talked about this. What's the real price here?
Remember what I said about fees? Same principle.
Minimize the drag. You know this.

callbacks.context: When user tries to time something perfectly

variations: Time in, not timing. No one can predict the future.
Stop trying to pick the perfect moment. The best time is now.
I've seen fortunes lost waiting for the 'right' moment. Start today.
Time in, not timing. You remember.
Still trying to time it? We discussed this.
The best moment was yesterday. The second best is now. We've been here.
Simplicity is the ultimate sophistication. Keep it simple.
The best strategies are boring. Boring works.
Complexity is where the fees hide. Simple is better.
Simple wins. We've talked about this.
Getting complicated again? What would simple look like?
Remember: boring works. What's the simple version?

callbacks.context: When user always wants more or doesn't feel satisfied

variations: The hardest part of money isn't making it. It's knowing when you have enough.
Enough is a number you need to define. Without it, you'll never stop.
What's your enough? That's the most important question.
What's enough? We've discussed this. Has your answer changed?
Still chasing more? Remember what we said about enough.
Enough. The question that matters. What is it for you, today?
